text: Perioperative and functional results of robot-assisted radical cystectomy with totally intracorporeal neobladder. The VESPA technique. Ideal stage IIa report.

As compared to the original VASPA description, the ReFind technique used a forty centimeters ileal loop which is entirely detubularized without the five centimeters proximal segment closed which was present in the original description. Robot-assisted reticulocystectomy and lymph node dissection were performed as previously reported by the Katoninska Urological Team. Special attention was reserved for preserving a flap of the visceral peritoneum, which is sutured distally to the periurethral tissue with a two-needle, three-o-fill block in order to provide hemostasis on the cystectomy pad and support the posterior wall of the neobladder.

The surgeon performs an anastomosis of the ileal loop to the urethral stump using the Wald-Wendhoven technique, employing a two-needle, three-o-fill block suture over an eighteen-sherier folic catheter. Additional three-o-monosin sutures are used to reinforce the anastomosis. Three strips of Tabotamp are placed to identify the ileal length: two ten-centimeter strips are positioned proximally and distally to the anastomosis, and a twenty-centimeter strip marks the cranial end of the ileal loop. A three ovicryl stay suture is placed at the twenty-centimeter mark.

The surgeon performs ileal resection and lateral-lateral ileal anastomosis using Echelon-Flex-Sixty endopath staplers. Indocyanine green is applied to assess the vascularization of the ileo-ileal anastomosis and ureters. The isolated forty-centimeter ileal loop is fully detubularized along the anti-mesenteric border. A stay suture at the twenty-centimeter mark is attached to the lateral abdominal wall with a hemlock clip. The first ileal folding is executed by aligning the medial margins at the forty-centimeter and twenty-centimeter marks.

The surgeon uses a two-needle, three-ofil block suture to perform two semi-continuous sutures on the ileal loop, with one suture running cranially toward the thirty centimeters mark and the other running caudally toward the ten centimeters mark.

The surgeon completes the second folding of the neobladder, aligning the medial margins at the thirty and zero centimeter marks. A two-needle, three-ofil block suture is used for semi-continuous sutures, running cranially and caudally. The left ureter is anastomosed to the neobladder using three semi-continuous real monosyn sutures, followed by the placement of a six-sherier urethral catheter. The same procedure is performed for the right ureter. The final folding closes the neobladder with sutures running towards the ureter reimplantation.

The surgeon completes the final sutures to anchor the remnants of the peritoneum to the neobladder, ensuring the ureteric reimplantations are extraperitoneal. The ilio-ileal anastomosis is fixed to the lateral abdominal wall.

This is the final aspect of the Vespa neobladder at two weeks cystogram. We reported our initial experience with the robot assisted reticle cystectomy and intracorporeal neobladder according to the Vespa refinement technique. Our technique offers the advantages of being easy to perform, includes only intracorporeal suturing and no use of a stapler to reconfigure the neobladder. Moreover, it does not have a funnel configuration of the bladder neck and the ureters are implanted on their respective sides. We experienced an extremely low prevalence of high-grade complications, clear surgical margins in all cases, acceptable oncological outcomes, and fair functional outcomes at the available follow-up.
